pattern: The one absent value in a 0..n permutation is recovered in linear time and constant space by subtracting the running array sum from the known full sum, or equivalently by XOR-cancelling indices against values.
hints: If no number were missing the array would hold every value from 0 to n exactly once; you only need to find which single value is absent.
The sum 0+1+...+n has a closed form, and the present numbers can be summed in one pass; their difference is the gap.
Compute n*(n+1)/2 for n equal to the array length, subtract the sum of the array, and return the result; XORing all indices and all values together also leaves only the missing number.

edge_inputs.description: missing value sits in the middle of the range
top of range is the gap
scrambled full range missing one interior value
single element, zero is present so one is missing
single element where zero is the missing value
zero itself is the absent number
largest value n is the absent number

stress.description: large shuffled 0..n range with one removed value, answer stays unique by construction
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    full = list(range(n + 1))
    miss = rng.randrange(0, n + 1)
    nums = [x for x in full if x != miss]
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
